experience.title: Postbaccalaureate Research Assistant
experience.organization: Center for Astronomy, Space Science & Astrophysics (CASSA), IUB

supervisor.name: Dr. Syed Ashraf Uddin
supervisor.title: Associate Professor & Core Member, CASSA, IUB

points: Working on fundamental cosmological problems related to Type Ia Supernovae (SNe Ia) as 'standardizable candles' for measuring cosmic expansion, dark energy, and the Hubble tension (5σ discrepancy in H₀ measurements)
Host Galaxy Correlation: Investigating the effect of host galaxy age on the luminosity of SNe Ia
Local Environment Analysis: Analyzing the impact of the local environment on SN Ia luminosity
Calibration Comparisons: Comparing SN Ia luminosity calibration across various light-curve fitters
Standard Candle Improvement: Improving SN Ia standardization through analysis of host galaxy spectra
Pipeline Development: Building a robust computational pipeline for spectral analysis of SNe Ia

experience.title: Postbaccalaureate Research Intern
experience.organization: Center for Astronomy, Space Science & Astrophysics (CASSA), IUB
points: RGC v1.0: Joined near completion; currently addressing peer reviewer comments from the journal committee and assisting in dataset re-preparation for resubmission to Astronomy & Astrophysics
RGC v2.0 Project: Developing hierarchical radio AGN classifier for bent morphologies (WAT, NAT) using 20,000+ images from LOFAR/LoTSS (144 MHz) and VLA/FIRST (1.4 GHz) surveys
Implementing semi-supervised learning frameworks and spurious source filtering algorithms for radio astronomical surveys
Vision LLM for Multi-Wavelength Astronomy: Building multimodal AI system ingesting galaxy images across optical, IR, radio, and X-ray bands for automated astrophysical characterization
Developing preprocessing pipeline: source extraction, image cutout generation, resolution matching, noise filtering, and normalization
Collaborating with international astronomy research teams; utilizing HPC resources for large-scale data processing
Gaining full research pipeline experience: data acquisition, preprocessing, model development, validation, and manuscript preparation using astroquery, astropy, and APLpy

experience.title: Research Collaborator
experience.organization: Young Learners' Research Lab, Department of CSE, RUET
points: Leading multiple machine learning research projects focusing on astronomical transient classification and lunar hyperspectral analysis
Developing LunaXNet framework: hybrid CNN-Transformer architecture for mineralogical segmentation, quantification, and interpretation from Chandrayaan-1 M3 hyperspectral data (85 bands, 430–3000 nm)
Implementing Grad-CAM based interpretability analysis for olivine, pyroxene, and plagioclase identification on the lunar surface
Co-authoring research on medical image classification using GANs with interpretable frameworks
Contributing to transformer-based NLP research for spam detection using BERT and GPT-2 architectures
Mentoring junior students in machine learning concepts and research methodologies

experience.title: Undergraduate Thesis Researcher
experience.organization: Department of Electrical & Computer Engineering, RUET

supervisor.name: Fariya Tabassum
supervisor.title: Assistant Professor & Head, ECE Department, RUET

experience.thesis_title: PIE-ADA: Physics-Informed Ensemble with Adaptive Data Augmentation for Photometric Transient Classification
points: Developed PIE-ADA, a comprehensive ML framework for 14-class astronomical transient classification using the PLAsTiCC dataset (7,848 original samples augmented to 8,148; 1.4M+ time-series observations)
Implemented astrophysically constrained synthetic data generation: cosmological time dilation, wavelength-dependent interstellar extinction, correlated noise injection, and observation phase shifting
Extracted 271 multi-scale features from six photometric passbands covering statistical, temporal, peak, color, and frequency-domain properties
Systematically benchmarked LightGBM, XGBoost, Random Forest, Extra Trees, Neural Networks, Gradient Boosting, AdaBoost, SVM, Logistic Regression, LSTM, and CNN
Addressed severe class imbalance (<1% for some classes) using SMOTE, ADASYN, class weighting, and physics-informed augmentation
Achieved weighted log-loss of 0.5767 (±0.0089) and 80.36% accuracy; 24–33% improvement over baseline algorithms
Full pipeline completes in <37 minutes; per-object classification in <0.05 seconds — suitable for real-time LSST alert processing

Classified 14 transient types: Type Ia/Ib/Ic/II supernovae, kilonovae, superluminous supernovae, pair-instability supernovae, AGN, tidal disruption events, RR Lyrae, M-dwarf flares, eclipsing binaries
